results.title: Minder project architecture plan
results.content: Minder is a Full Agentic RAG MCP Server built with Python + LangGraph + Milvus + NV-Embed-v2 + Nemotron-3 Nano-4B GGUF. Architecture: MCP Transport Layer → LangGraph Orchestrator (Planning, Retriever, Reranker, Reasoning, LLM, Guard, Verification, Evaluator nodes) → Multi-tier Data Layer (Vector/Milvus, Relational/SQLite, Document/JSONL, Graph, Cache). 8 memory stores: Skill, History, Error, Session, Metadata, Document, Rule, Feedback. 5 implementation phases: Foundation → Agentic Pipeline → Advanced Retrieval + Knowledge Graph → Scale + Production → Self-Improvement. Plan stored at docs/PLAN.md.

results.title: Minder confirmed decisions v2
results.content: Minder plan v2 confirmed: Team multi-user (email+username from git), Auth mandatory (API key + JWT + RBAC), SSE transport from Phase 1, NV-Embed-v2 GGUF + Nemotron-3 GGUF mandatory (offline-first, OpenAI fallback optional), Docker sandbox mandatory for verification (subprocess dev mode), Skills seeded from external GitHub repo, CI/CD from Phase 1 (GitHub Actions + GitHub Releases + GitHub Packages/ghcr.io), Knowledge Graph Phase 3 confirmed. 9 stores total (added User Store). Plan at docs/PLAN.md v2.0 status: DECISIONS CONFIRMED.

results.title: Minder switched to Qwen plan split
results.content: Updated the Minder planning docs to use Qwen/Qwen3-Embedding-0.6B quantized GGUF for embeddings and Qwen3.5-0.8B quantized GGUF for the local LLM, both intended to run through llama.cpp via llama-cpp-python. Split docs/PLAN.md into an index plus docs/plan/01-06 focused documents covering scope, architecture, data/tools, workflow governance, implementation phases, and operations/delivery.

results.title: Instruction updates for memory lifecycle commands
results.content: Updated the source instruction layer to match new memory lifecycle commands. Refreshed .agents workflow guidance so agents now distinguish between coder memory store for new knowledge, coder memory verify for reconfirming an existing memory, coder memory supersede or store --replace-active for replacing active truth, and coder memory audit when conflicts or stale active memories may remain. Also updated the coder-cli skill and architecture doc to describe the new memory endpoints, conflict-aware retrieval behavior, and lifecycle-aware context injection expectations.

results.title: LINE configure step uses split checklist cards
results.content: Refactored LINE StepConfigureChannel to match the WhatsApp readiness-card pattern. Replaced the old single checkbox with three atom-based checklist cards backed by per-item state in LineConnect, enabled Next only when all checklist items are checked, moved checklist styling into channelThemes, and updated lineSetup i18n keys across en/vi/ja/id/zh.
